channel.title: Julius Kibunjia's Blog
channel.description: Personal blog of Programmer Julius Kibunjia

item.title: Building a Movie Catalog with Obsidian: Part 1
item.description: Obsidian is a great tool for note taking but an even better one for cataloging different forms of data. In this article, I'll describe how I use it to track movies.

item.title: Extracting Structured Data from PDF Files
item.description: In this article I will go over the details of a recent project involving extracting data from a PDF Swahili-English dictionary.

item.title: Using A* and Python to Solve a Puzzle
item.description: In this article we'll be trying to solve the puzzle game flood using Python. The game is played on a grid of cells that can be of any fixed number of colors.

item.title: Web Scraping on the Shell
item.description: Often times there's data on the internet we'd like to get our hands on. In most cases I'd start on a Python script with the usual suspects requests, beautifulsoup4, json or csv. This works well butwhen the task in question only needs to be run once, it would be alot easier to run a few commands on the shell.

item.title: Using a Genetic Algorithm to Guess Arithmetic Equations in Julia
item.description: Genetic algorithms try to replicate the mechanisms of biological evolution to solve problems. In this article we'll use a somewhat useless example problem to demonstrate how to design and implement a genetic algorithm in Julia which I have been trying to learn recently.

item.title: Tips For An Improved Arch Linux Experience
item.description: Arch is great but can require some effort to configure. Here are a couple of tips.

item.title: Implementing Asemica: A Markov Chain Based Cipher
item.description: Factor implementation of a book keyed cipher, Asemica.

item.title: Querying Pacman with Datalog
item.description: Using Datalog to query pacman dependencies.

item.title: Generating Mazes with Haskell
item.description: Implementing mazes from 'Mazes for Programmers' book.

item.title: Makefile Madness
item.description: Tricks for transforming Make into a command runner.

item.title: Writing a Command for Ranger
item.description: Using Python to write a custom command for Ranger file manager.

item.title: Data flow on the shell
item.description: Different ways to move data around the shell.
